meaning: n.(专注自我而与现实隔绝的)孤独症患者(常指儿童)
examples: This was also found in high-functioning men with some autistic spectrum symptoms, who may tend to avoid eye contact.
Autistic spectrum disorders often result in major difficulties in comprehending verbal information and speech processing.
This is difficult to scientifically quantify as such extra-sensory stimuli vary greatly from one autistic individual to another.
Autistic spectrum disorders, including Asperger's syndrome, are thought to affect about 1% of the population worldwide.
That's because until recently, very little information on autistic children has been widely available.
